courseleaf.title: Linguistics, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
Minor

Linguistics offers two minor programs:

  • Linguistics (General) provides the student with basic knowledge of language structure and linguistic analysis.
  • Linguistics for Language Teachers especially complements the major in English with the Teaching Area of emphasis; it is also of relevance to students interested in teaching foreign languages.

Minor Advisor

Contact the advising office.
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
LIN 001Introduction to Linguistics4
or LIN 001Y Introduction to Linguistics
LIN 103ALinguistic Analysis I: Phonetics, Phonology, Morphology4
or LIN 103AV Linguistic Analysis I: Phonetics, Phonology, & Morphology
LIN 103BLinguistic Analysis II: Morphology, Syntax, Semantics4
Choose one:4
LIN 111
Introduction to Phonological Theory
LIN 112
Phonetics
LIN 121
Morphology
LIN 131
Introduction to Syntactic Theory
LIN 141
Semantics
LIN 151
Historical Linguistics
LIN 152
Language Universals & Typology
Choose additional units from upper division Linguistics courses and other upper division courses listed in the major requirements in consultation with an advisor.8
Total Units24
courseleaf.quickviewtext: Are you interested in the study of language structure? The General Linguistics minor provides students with basic knowledge of language structure and linguistic analysis.
